evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight new skill. A pre-scaffolded project exists at /Users/nic/src/ai-home/pm-framework/.worktrees/plugin-conversion/skills/test-fixtures/scaffolded-project/. Use that directory as your working directory for all file operations — treat all relative paths as relative to that directory.

Run /preflight new adr. When the skill asks questions, provide these answers:
- Context: We need to decide how to handle background job processing. Currently jobs run inline in the request, causing timeouts on heavy operations.
- Decision drivers: Reliability (jobs must not be lost on crash), observability (need job status visibility), team expertise (team knows Redis well), operational simplicity.
- Option 1 — Sidekiq (Redis-backed): pros: team familiarity, proven at scale, good UI. Cons: requires Redis, Ruby-centric.
- Option 2 — Database-backed queue (GoodJob): pros: no extra infra, uses existing PostgreSQL. Cons: higher DB load, less battle-tested at scale.
- Decision: Sidekiq, because reliability and observability outweigh operational cost of Redis which we already run.
- Consequences: Good: reliable async jobs with retries. Good: existing Redis instance reused. Bad: another dependency on Redis uptime. Neutral: team training on Sidekiq patterns needed.
- Confirmation: Zero dropped jobs in first 30 days post-migration. Job queue depth monitored via Grafana alert.
- Slug: use-sidekiq-for-background-jobs
- Owner: backend-team
- Deciders: backend-team, platform-team
- Consulted: frontend-team
- Informed: all-engineers

After the skill writes the ADR and runs the post-creation review, save:
1. The generated ADR file content to outputs/adr-output.md
2. The post-creation review output (pass/fail summary) to outputs/review-summary.txt
evals.expected_output: A properly formatted MADR 4.0 ADR file with correct frontmatter, at least 2 options with pros/cons, decision outcome with consequences, confirmation criteria. Sequential ID (adr-003-...). Post-creation review runs and reports findings.

evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight new skill. A pre-scaffolded project exists at /Users/nic/src/ai-home/pm-framework/.worktrees/plugin-conversion/skills/test-fixtures/scaffolded-project/. Use that directory as your working directory for all file operations.

Run /preflight new requirements. When the skill asks questions, provide these answers:
- Problem statement: The platform needs a notification system. Users currently have no way to receive alerts about events relevant to them (new messages, task completions, system alerts).
- Personas: (1) End user — receives notifications, wants relevance and timeliness, non-technical. (2) Admin — configures notification channels and templates, technically sophisticated.
- Primary user journey: User receives a notification when a task assigned to them is completed. Trigger: task marked complete. Steps: system detects event, evaluates user preferences, delivers via configured channel. Success: user sees notification within 30 seconds. Failure: delivery fails — retry up to 3 times.
- Functional requirements: derive from the journey above.
- NFRs: notification delivery p95 < 30 seconds; system handles 10,000 notifications/minute; 99.9% delivery success rate over 30-day rolling window.
- Constraints: must support email and in-app channels; no SMS in v1.
- Assumptions: users have valid email on file.
- Out of scope: push notifications (mobile), SMS.
- Success measures: delivery latency p95 < 30s; 99.9% delivery rate measured via delivery receipts.

After the skill writes the requirements doc and runs the post-creation review, save:
1. The generated requirements file content to outputs/requirements-output.md
2. The post-creation review summary to outputs/review-summary.txt
evals.expected_output: A requirements doc with EARS-formatted requirements (FR-NNN IDs sequential from FR-001), measurable NFRs, proper frontmatter. Post-creation review runs. Any auto-fixed errors reported.

evals.prompt:
You are testing the /preflight new skill's initialization guard. Run /preflight new adr in a directory that has NOT been scaffolded — use /tmp/preflight-uninitialized-test/ as your working directory (create it fresh with mkdir -p). Do not create any .preflight/ directory there.

The skill should detect that .preflight/_templates/ does not exist and stop with a clear error message telling the user to run /preflight scaffold first.

Save the skill's response/output to outputs/guard-output.txt
evals.expected_output: Skill stops at Step 0 with a message telling the user to run /preflight scaffold first. Does NOT proceed to ask elicitation questions.
